notes: Arturia Modular V emulates the Moog System 55 modular synthesizer, the towering patchable system that defined early electronic music through artists like Wendy Carlos, Keith Emerson, and Rick Wakeman. At its core are three voltage-controlled oscillators, the iconic Moog transistor ladder 24dB/oct low-pass filter, dual envelope generators, two LFOs, a ring modulator, a sample-and-hold circuit, and a spring reverb. The virtual patch bay allows near-infinite routing possibilities beyond the pre-patched signal flow. The Moog ladder filter's warm, creamy, self-oscillating character is the sonic centrepiece and the first parameter to automate in any performance context.

Expression.description: Expression — scales output level for dynamics

Modulation.description: Modulation wheel — typically routed to filter cutoff or VCO pitch

Portamento Time.description: Portamento time — Moog-style glide between pitches

VCF Cutoff.description: Moog ladder filter cutoff frequency — the warm, creamy 24dB/oct low-pass; the defining sonic control

VCF Resonance.description: Filter resonance/emphasis — adds the characteristic Moog resonant peak; self-oscillates as a sine wave at maximum

VCF Env Amount.description: Filter envelope amount — how much EG1 opens or closes the filter cutoff

VCO Mix.description: VCO mix balance — relative levels of the three oscillators in the mixer

Attack.description: EG1 attack time — envelope applied to both filter and VCA

LFO Rate.description: LFO rate — speed of the primary low-frequency oscillator

LFO Depth.description: LFO depth — modulation amount applied to pitch, filter, or PWM

VCO1 Tune.description: VCO1 fine tune — pitch offset for detuning against VCO2 and VCO3

VCO2 Tune.description: VCO2 fine tune — detuning creates beating and fatness

Noise Level.description: Noise generator level — white noise for percussion, wind effects, and S&H input

Spring Reverb.description: Built-in spring reverb level — classic Moog warm hall reverb character
